releases.label: 4XMM-DR13
releases.summary: Thirteenth incremental release of the fourth XMM-Newton serendipitous source catalogue (July 2023) — 656,997 unique X-ray sources from pointed observations public through 2022; superseded by 4XMM-DR14

releases.label: 4XMM-DR14
releases.summary: Final incremental 4XMM release — 692,109 unique sources (1,035,832 detections) from 13,864 XMM-Newton observations public by 2023-12-31, 1,383 deg2 unique sky area; companion stacked catalogue 4XMM-DR14s holds 427,524 unique sources from 1,751 overlapping-observation groups. Superseded by 5XMM-DR15.

releases.label: 5XMM-DR15
releases.summary: Fifth-generation XMM-Newton serendipitous source catalogue, released by ESA 2026-06-09 — 818,656 unique X-ray sources (2,578,752 detections and upper limits) from 14,616 EPIC observations made 2000-01-19 to 2024-10-14 (public by 2024-10-31), ~1,397 deg2 net sky area. Stacked-only catalogue (improved stacking includes non-overlapping observations); full archive bulk reprocessing with SAS v21 / pipeline v21.51. Reference paper not yet on ADS at seed time.
